## childProcessToPostMessage() function

Creates a PostMessageEndpoint from a Node.js Child Process with IPC enabled.

This adapter converts a Node.js child process into a PostMessage-compatible interface, enabling use with remobj's PostMessage-based APIs. Requires the child process to be created with IPC enabled (fork() or spawn with stdio: 'ipc').

**Signature:**

```typescript
export declare function childProcessToPostMessage(childProcess: any): PostMessageEndpoint;
```

</th><th>

</th><th>

</th></tr></thead>

childProcess


</td><td>

</td><td>

The child process instance with IPC enabled


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

PostMessageEndpoint

A PostMessageEndpoint interface for the child process

## Example


```typescript
import { fork } from 'child_process'
import { childProcessToPostMessage } from '@remobj/node/adapter/node'

const child = fork('./worker.js')
const endpoint = childProcessToPostMessage(child)
endpoint.postMessage({ command: 'start', data: [1, 2, 3] })
```
